codeReview.summary: The migration removed `Result<T, String>` across the targeted backend/workflow, Axum handler, and Tauri command paths, but introduced a behavior regression in snapshot validation error classification.

issues.description: In `validate_mappings`, malformed snapshot mapping errors are returned as `Err(format!(...).into())` (lines 299, 310, 320), which now converts to `CsvAlignError::Internal` via `impl From<String> for CsvAlignError` (`src/backend/error.rs:86-88`). These are user-input validation failures and should be `BadInput`/400; with the current code they become internal/500 when bubbled through `error.into_response()` in `src/api/handlers.rs` (e.g. line 294).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used `cargo test /Users/vriesd/projects/csv-align/tests/backend_workflow_integration.rs -j 4`, which Cargo treats as a name filter, so no test cases executed. The rust-worker skill could explicitly document the correct form for single integration test binaries (`cargo test --test <name>`) to avoid false confidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification entry for that command states: "the command used a path filter so no individual tests ran".

sharedStateObservations.observation: Current shared docs do not explicitly warn that `String`/`&str` conversions into `CsvAlignError` map to `Internal`, which is risky during migrations where `.into()` may be used on user-input validation paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/backend/error.rs:86-94 maps `From<String>` and `From<&str>` to `CsvAlignError::Internal`; commit 0a684c6 uses `.into()` for snapshot validation failures in src/backend/comparison_snapshot.rs:299/310/320.

summary: Reviewed worker session c17a4724-9e8f-43e8-8962-d86ae56248e7 and commit 0a684c6. While the broad `Result<_, String>` migration is present, one blocking regression remains: invalid snapshot mapping input is now classified as internal server error instead of bad input due to `String -> CsvAlignError::Internal` conversion.
